title: Raymond H. Myers' Contribution to Experimental Design
description: A flowchart illustrating the foundational concepts and workflow in experimental design as formalized by Raymond H. Myers, connecting them to the ANOVA process.
relationships: Defining the problem leads to selecting measurable outcomes
Selected factors influence the choice of experimental design
The design dictates the randomization and data collection procedure
Collected data is analyzed using an ANOVA model
The ANOVA results are used for statistical inference about factor effects
